key_clauses.text_excerpt: Provider may process Customer Data in any jurisdiction where Provider or its subprocessors maintain facilities.
In no event shall Provider's aggregate liability exceed the fees paid in the twelve months preceding the claim.
This Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one-year terms unless either party provides 30 days written notice.
Confidential Information means any information disclosed orally without requiring written confirmation or marking.
The obligations under this Agreement shall survive for a period of five years from the date of disclosure.
Information independently developed by Receiving Party without reference to Confidential Information shall not be subject to this Agreement.
Employee shall not engage in any competing business within a 100-mile radius for a period of 24 months following termination.
All inventions, whether or not related to Employee's duties, conceived during the term of employment shall be the sole property of Employer.
Any disputes shall be resolved through binding arbitration under AAA rules, with the Employee waiving the right to jury trial.
Vendor shall comply with all applicable provisions of HIPAA and shall execute a Business Associate Agreement prior to accessing any PHI.
Vendor shall indemnify Client against any claims arising from Vendor's breach of security obligations, including regulatory fines and penalties.
Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon 90 days written notice, subject to payment for services rendered.
All Customer Data shall be stored and processed exclusively within data centers located in the continental United States.
Customer shall have the right to audit Provider's security practices annually, with 30 days advance notice.
Provider guarantees 99.95% uptime measured monthly, with service credits of 10% of monthly fees for each 0.1% below the target.
Provider acknowledges that Customer is subject to OCC, FDIC, and state banking regulations and shall cooperate with all regulatory examinations.
Licensee shall not use the Models to generate content that competes with Licensor's own products or to train competing models.
Licensee retains ownership of outputs generated using the Models, subject to Licensor's underlying intellectual property rights.
Usage is subject to published rate limits, which Licensor may modify with 30 days notice.
Profits and losses shall be allocated 60/40 in favor of Partner A until Partner A's capital contribution has been returned, thereafter 50/50.
Partner A shall have sole authority over all operational decisions with expenditures up to $500,000 without requiring Partner B consent.
Upon a partner's withdrawal, the remaining partner has 180 days to purchase the departing partner's interest at book value, excluding goodwill.
Processor shall process Personal Data only on documented instructions from Controller and shall implement appropriate technical and organizational measures per Article 32.
Transfers to the United States shall be governed by Standard Contractual Clauses (Module 2) as adopted by the European Commission Decision 2021/914.
Processor shall not engage sub-processors without prior specific written authorization from Controller and shall impose equivalent data protection obligations.
All change orders must be approved in writing by Owner before work commences, with pricing adjustments based on actual cost plus 15% overhead.
Contractor shall pay $5,000 per calendar day for each day completion extends beyond the scheduled completion date, capped at 10% of contract value.
Contractor warrants all work for a period of two years from substantial completion, excluding normal wear and tear.
Franchisee is granted a non-exclusive territory within a 3-mile radius, provided that Franchisor retains the right to operate or license additional locations.
Franchisee shall pay a continuing royalty of 6% of gross sales and a marketing contribution of 2% of gross sales, due weekly.
Franchisor may terminate immediately upon Franchisee's failure to cure a material breach within 15 days of written notice.
Provider reserves the right to modify these terms at any time by posting updated terms on its website, with continued use constituting acceptance.
Upon termination, Customer may request data export in CSV format within 30 days, after which all Customer Data will be permanently deleted.
Provider's total liability shall not exceed $100 regardless of the nature of the claim or the amount of fees paid.
All intellectual property developed jointly shall be co-owned, with each party having the right to license the IP independently without accounting to the other party.
Party A shall be responsible for all FDA regulatory submissions and shall bear the costs thereof, with Party B providing technical support.
Party B shall receive milestone payments of $2M upon IND filing, $5M upon Phase II completion, and $15M upon FDA approval.
Base rent shall increase by 3.5% annually, compounded, with additional adjustments tied to CPI if CPI exceeds 4% in any calendar year.
CEO of Tenant personally guarantees all obligations under this Lease for the full 7-year term, surviving any corporate dissolution.
Tenant may not assign or sublease without Landlord's prior written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld.
Consultant shall provide strategic advisory services as reasonably requested by Client, with deliverables to be mutually agreed upon.
Client shall pay Consultant $350 per hour, invoiced monthly, with payment due within 30 days of invoice date.
All deliverables created under this Agreement shall be works made for hire and shall belong exclusively to Client.
All parts shall conform to IATF 16949 quality standards, with defect rates not exceeding 50 PPM. Supplier shall maintain PPAP documentation.
Neither party shall be liable for delays caused by events beyond reasonable control, provided the affected party gives notice within 48 hours.
Supplier shall bear all costs associated with product recalls attributable to defective parts, including Buyer's administrative costs, logistics, and customer remediation expenses.
